steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: Substitute $x = 0$ into the equation: $$y = 4 \times 2^0 = 4 \times 1 = 4$$ The graph passes through $(0, 4)$ on the $y$-axis.

steps.title: Determine exponential growth and asymptotic behavior
steps.content:
The equation has the form $y = a \cdot b^x$ with $a = 4$ and base $b = 2 > 1$, so the function grows exponentially.

Check another point: at $x = 1$, $$y = 4 \times 2^1 = 8$$
For negative $x$-values, as $x \to -\infty$, the term $2^x \to 0$, so $y \to 0$. This means the graph approaches the $x$-axis as a horizontal asymptote but never touches it. The function is always positive.

steps.title: Match the graph to the characteristics
steps.content:
The correct graph must show:
  • A curve passing through $(0, 4)$
  • Steep exponential growth to the right
  • Asymptotic behavior approaching the $x$-axis on the left
  • Always above the $x$-axis (no $x$-intercept)
That matches choice D — D is correct.
